title: This Unique Nutrient Can Detox Seed Oils

chunks.title: Introduction to Odd Chain Fatty Acids
chunks.summary: The discussion begins with the introduction of odd chain fatty acids, particularly pentadecanoic acid (C15), which is commonly found in dairy. The speaker highlights recent studies that suggest C15 levels are inversely correlated with ferroptosis, a type of cell death related to lipid peroxidation.
chunks.transcript: have you seen the research on the odd chain fatty acids yes these are really cool the fatty 15 stuff yes like pentadecanoic acid which is C15 yeah I've had those guys on the show talking about the research yeah so there's a recent study that I came across showing that C15 which is pentadecanoic acid and that fatty acid is found in Dairy right fermented dairy yes it is a it is a very strong indicator of dairy consumption in humans and levels of C15 are inversely correlated with degree of ferroptosis in humans and ferroptosis is apoptosis

chunks.title: Ferroptosis and Dietary Implications
chunks.summary: The video elaborates on the concept of ferroptosis, explaining its relation to dietary fats, particularly polyunsaturated fats. It suggests that higher levels of C15 might protect against oxidative stress caused by these fats, pointing out the irony that dairy fats, which could be protective, are often vilified.
chunks.transcript: program cell death related to lipid peroxidation interesting so ferroptosis is this mechanistic cell death that is related to excess seed oils in our cells if you stuff your cells full of polyan citrated fatty acids whether it's linolic acid or other polyan citrated fats and your body cannot manage the oxidative liability of those they will commit ferroptosis which is iron the Ferro Ferro is the iron you know so it is an oxidated stress a lipid peroxidation derived cell death the more C15 you have in your cells the less your cells do that so it looks like the potential here is that these odd chain fatty acids may protect us from the oxidative stress the oxidative liability of poly insyriated fats in our diets and yet which fats are getting vilified Dairy fats butter the ones that help you yeah it it's funny I've for years

chunks.title: Choosing Healthy Fats
chunks.summary: The speaker provides practical advice on selecting healthy fats, promoting unsalted cultured butter and tallow as beneficial options. They discuss the importance of consuming fermented dairy for its odd chain fatty acids and offer insights on detoxifying from seed oils in the diet.
chunks.transcript: recommended that you get unsalted Cary gold carye gold has its problems I've I've talked about them as ad AUM I helped to do a a protest to get them like 10% not grass-fed in Winter fine whatever it's widely available the reason unsalted Cary gold versus salted salted Cary gold butter is mechanically separated butter unsalted is fermented it's cultured cultured butter has the odd chain fats in it yeah it tastes different but it's better for you right so guys if you're listening there's hundreds of grass-fed butter brands thank God right and car Gold's one that you can get at Safeway in a food desert and it's going to be pretty good but it's not the most perfect you could get so I will do that and I do a lot of Tallow I cook with Tallow um or duck fat sometimes people always ask me how do you detox seed oils potentially you eat fermented butter you know or you eat fermented dairy for the Ain fatty acids and you just stop eating it it's and I

chunks.title: MCT Oil and Digestive Effects
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts towards MCT oil, with humorous anecdotes about its digestive effects when poorly processed. The speaker shares experiences with different types of MCT oil and its relation to odd chain fatty acids.

chunks.title: Health Benefits of Odd Chain Fatty Acids
chunks.summary: The video emphasizes the health benefits of odd chain fatty acids, noting their association with longevity and better health outcomes. The speaker criticizes the lack of focus on these acids in the supplement industry and within the longevity space, encouraging a return to natural sources like animal foods.
chunks.transcript: for health they are really important for health and Beyond the ferroptosis research there is observational research which consistently Associates higher levels of both odd chain fatty acids c-15 and c17 with better outcomes in terms of I think overall length of life but no one in the longevity space is talking about oine fatty acids they're a big deal and it's interesting to me that a lot of the people in the longevity space are taking a lot of supplements for things that just happen to occur in animal Foods creatine toine vitamin B12 nobody's doing oain fatty acids yet but they will like there are so many compounds that have longevity benefits that seem to occur in animal Foods you could just eat a steak guys I'm saying oh as long as you put some cultured butter on top yeah make it even more
